cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. Let me read through the passage again to get the details right. The passage says that Babbage was working in London for four years after exhibiting his invention in 1822. Then his health broke down, and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources, many bills were unpaid, his chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with expensive tools. For one year, no work was done. So the question is asking why he failed to continue in 1828. The options are A) poor health, B) ran out of money, C) co-workers argued and left, D) spent all on bills. The key part is "he was at the end of his resources" and "many bills remained unpaid." Also, his co-workers left. So multiple factors here. The question is what was the main reason. The problem is whether it's B or C. The passage says that after returning, he was out of resources (money) and had unpaid bills. Then his co-workers quarreled and left. So the main issue might be both money and workers. But the question asks for the main reason. The first thing mentioned is being at the end of his resources, which is money. Then the co-workers leaving is another issue. However, the next year no work was done during that period. The answer options are separate. The question is which one is the main reason for failing to continue. The answer might be B (money) because that's the first problem mentioned, and the co-workers leaving might be a result of the financial issues. Also, when he couldn't pay bills, maybe he couldn't pay his workers, leading to them leaving. So the primary cause is B. Alternatively, maybe the quarreling led them to leave, but the passage says he was at the end of his resources,
